ops.title: Fleet installer rollout: post-merge follow-ups
ops.description:
After the canonical-installer PR chain merges (cc-skills#2 -> cc-interact#1 -> cc-review/cc-runtime/slop-cop#1), four follow-ups remain:
1. Bump CC_SKILLS_REF in cc-interact plugin-template/render.sh from the provisional PR-head 7f143612aa5bb563f534aae36f6848d0ff6d2975 to the cc-skills#2 MERGE sha (render.sh carries the reminder comment; cc-interact#1 body gates its own merge on deciding this).
2. Finalize cc-context's provenance stamp: plugin/scripts/install-binary.sh line 2 says @pending — run render.sh --sync-scripts plugin/ from the merged plugin-template and commit (also refreshes every consumer's stamp to the merge sha; drift grep: grep -r 'canonical: cc-skills/plugins/repo-bootstrap@').
3. Swap the cc-skills copy of the slop-cop plugin (plugins/slop-cop) to a canonical LATEST render + SKILL.md/hook rewiring — blocked until slop-cop#1 merges AND its next release publishes bare assets + --version (that repo auto-releases on push to main, so the gap is minutes).
4. Optional: Windows parity — install-binary.ps1 keeps the old contract (plain zip download, no symlink/checksum); canonical template has no ps1 twin today.
Merge order is load-bearing: cc-skills#2 first (plugin-template fetch tiers 2-3 resolve against merged cc-skills).

ops.text: CC_SKILLS_REF pinned to a sha reachable from cc-skills main
no @pending provenance stamps remain across the fleet
cc-skills slop-cop plugin resolves its binary via the canonical installer on a brew-less machine
